text.title: Pricing Strategy Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Pricing Strategy. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Floor = direct cost + amortised fixed cost per customer / month. Pricing below floor = subsidising customers.
rule.rationale: Below-floor pricing scales the loss with growth.

rule.statement: Ceiling = customer-perceived value from ≥10 interviews (WTP). Above ceiling = no buyers.
rule.rationale: Setting price above WTP starves the funnel.

rule.statement: Pick exactly one of: subscription | usage | hybrid | one-time. Mixed-without-declaration = customer confusion.

rationale.source: Pricing literature (Tom Nagle, Pricing With Confidence)

rule.statement: Every price change has a 60-day test plan: conversion + churn + ARPU. Reverting before 60 days = noise-driven.
rule.rationale: Reverts inside 60 days are driven by noise, not signal.

rule.statement: Existing customers grandfathered ≥6 months on price increases. Forced migration = mass churn.
Pricing owner is named on the spec; not 'marketing' or 'team'.
If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-ops-pricing-strategy.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
